stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Linens are woven flax fabrics with a variety of uses.
  • Damask linen is a handwoven fabric produced in Northern Ireland.
  • It has an intricate jacquard pattern and is primarily used for fine table coverings.
  • Burlap linen is a machine-made fabric produced in factories.
  • It has a coarse, loose weave and is primarily used to package goods and reinforce wall plaster.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two linens. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Damask linen and burlap linen are two different types of woven flax fabrics.
Burlap linen is machine-made, has a coarse weave, and is used to package goods and reinforce plaster.
Damask linen is a handwoven fabric with an intricate jacquard pattern, while burlap linen is machine-made with a coarse, loose weave.
Linens can be used in a variety of ways, from creating fine table coverings to packaging goods and reinforcing wall plaster.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The difference between the two different kinds of linen is emphasized.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice lists two kinds of linens but does not say how they are different from one another. Choice B is incorrect. This choice does not emphasize a difference between the two linens. It only describes burlap linen. Choice D is incorrect. This choice does not emphasize a difference between the two linens.
